levels.title: Beginner (Principiante)
levels.milestone: Handle simple daily exchanges using the present tense, correct gender agreement and the fused prepositions.
canDo: Read any Italian word aloud correctly: c and g before e and i, the digraphs gli, gn and sc, and the double consonants that change meaning (fato vs fatto, nono vs nonno).
Conjugate the present of the three conjugations, parlare, credere and dormire, plus the -isc- pattern of capire and the irregulars essere, avere, andare, fare and stare.
Give every noun the right article and make every adjective agree in gender and number: il libro nuovo, la casa nuova, gli amici nuovi.
Use the preposizioni articolate without hesitating: vado al mercato, vengo dal lavoro, il libro è sul tavolo, abito nella città vecchia.
Introduce yourself, ask and answer with chi, che cosa, dove, quando, come, quanto and perché, count, tell the time and negate with non before the verb.

descriptors: I can recognise familiar words and very basic phrases concerning myself, my family and immediate concrete surroundings when people speak slowly and clearly.
I can understand familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues.
I can interact in a simple way provided the other person is prepared to repeat or rephrase things at a slower rate of speech and help me formulate what I'm trying to say.
I can ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.
I can use simple phrases and sentences to describe where I live and people I know.
I can write a short, simple postcard, for example sending holiday greetings.
I can fill in forms with personal details, for example entering my name, nationality and address on a hotel registration form.

exam.name: CILS A1
exam.description: The first rung of the CILS ladder, awarded by the Università per Stranieri di Siena. It tests five separate abilities, including a grammar and language-structures section that the other Italian exam families fold into reading. This checkpoint practises that five-part format and applies its per-ability pass rule.
sections: Analisi delle strutture di comunicazione, grammar and language structures, 12 points
exam.passRule: Cinque abilità, 12 punti ciascuna, 60 punti in totale. Sufficienza con almeno 7 punti in OGNI abilità e almeno 35 punti complessivi. Le abilità non si compensano fra loro.

levels.title: Elementary (Elementare)
levels.milestone: Tell what happened and what used to happen, using the passato prossimo, the imperfetto and the object pronouns.
canDo: Build the passato prossimo and pick the auxiliary correctly: ho mangiato but sono andato, with the participle agreeing with the subject after essere.
Use the imperfetto for description, habit, age and weather in the past, and combine it with the passato prossimo to tell a story: leggevo quando è arrivato.
Replace nouns with direct and indirect object pronouns in the right position, before the conjugated verb and attached to infinitives and imperatives.
Handle reflexive verbs across the day (mi alzo, ci vediamo, si sono sposati) and the particles ci and ne that Italian uses constantly.
Give instructions and directions with the imperative, and talk about plans with the futuro semplice.

descriptors: I can understand phrases and the highest frequency vocabulary related to areas of most immediate personal relevance, for example very basic personal and family information, shopping, local area, employment.
I can catch the main point in short, clear, simple messages and announcements.
I can read very short, simple texts.
I can find specific, predictable information in simple everyday material such as advertisements, prospectuses, menus and timetables, and I can understand short simple personal letters.
I can communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring a simple and direct exchange of information on familiar topics and activities.
I can handle very short social exchanges, even though I can't usually understand enough to keep the conversation going myself.
I can use a series of phrases and sentences to describe in simple terms my family and other people, living conditions, my educational background and my present or most recent job.
I can write short, simple notes and messages relating to matters in areas of immediate need.
I can write a very simple personal letter, for example thanking someone for something.

exam.name: CILS A2
exam.description: The second rung of the CILS ladder. A2 is also the level Italy requires for the EU long-term residence permit, the permesso di soggiorno UE per soggiornanti di lungo periodo, which is a different and lower bar than citizenship. Five abilities again, each scored and passed on its own.
sections: Analisi delle strutture di comunicazione, grammar and language structures, 12 points
exam.passRule: Cinque abilità, 12 punti ciascuna, 60 punti in totale. Sufficienza con almeno 7 punti in OGNI abilità e almeno 35 punti complessivi. Un punteggio alto in un'abilità non recupera un'abilità insufficiente.

levels.title: Intermediate (Intermedio)
levels.milestone: State and justify an opinion, hypothesise, and pass the B1 that Italian citizenship requires.
canDo: Use the condizionale for polite requests, advice and hypothesis: vorrei un caffè, dovresti riposare, andrei volentieri.
Use the congiuntivo after the verbs and conjunctions that demand it: penso che sia, voglio che tu venga, benché sia tardi, prima che parta.
Build the periodo ipotetico of possibility and of unreality: se avessi tempo, verrei; se avessi studiato, avrei passato l'esame.
Combine pronouns (glielo, me ne, ce li), use the relative pronouns che, cui and il quale, and handle the passive and the si passivante.
Write a structured letter, email or opinion text and take part in a real discussion, giving reasons with perché, quindi, però, anche se and infatti.

descriptors: I can understand the main points of clear standard speech on familiar matters regularly encountered in work, school, leisure and so on.
I can understand the main point of many radio or TV programmes on current affairs or topics of personal or professional interest when the delivery is relatively slow and clear.
I can understand texts that consist mainly of high frequency everyday or job-related language.
I can understand the description of events, feelings and wishes in personal letters.
I can deal with most situations likely to arise whilst travelling in an area where the language is spoken.
I can enter unprepared into conversation on topics that are familiar, of personal interest or pertinent to everyday life, for example family, hobbies, work, travel and current events.
I can connect phrases in a simple way in order to describe experiences and events, my dreams, hopes and ambitions.
I can briefly give reasons and explanations for opinions and plans, and narrate the plot of a book or film and describe my reactions.
I can write simple connected text on topics which are familiar or of personal interest.
I can write personal letters describing experiences and impressions.

exam.name: CILS UNO-B1
exam.description: The finish line. B1 is the level Italian citizenship by residence or by marriage requires, and CILS is one of the four CLIQ certifications the Ministero dell'Interno accepts. The exam is modular in the strict sense: five abilities, each scored separately, and a strong section never rescues a weak one. Be careful about one thing. A separate, shorter exam called CILS B1 Cittadinanza also exists, and the Università per Stranieri di Siena states that it covers a much narrower slice of competence, so it is valid for citizenship ONLY and is not accepted for work or for university study. It also has no carry-over: fail one section and you re-sit the whole thing. If you may ever need the certificate for a job or a degree, sit this standard CILS UNO-B1 instead. This checkpoint practises the standard five-part format and applies its per-ability pass rule.
sections: Analisi delle strutture di comunicazione, grammar and language structures, 20 points
exam.passRule: Prova modulare. Cinque abilità, 20 punti ciascuna, 100 punti in totale. Sufficienza con almeno 11 punti in OGNI abilità e almeno 55 punti complessivi, non con la media. Nelle prove CILS standard le abilità superate si capitalizzano per 18 mesi e si ripetono solo quelle insufficienti.
